front: What is the truth table for the logical connective AND (conjunction)?
back:
P | Q | P ∧ Q
T | T |   T
T | F |   F
F | T |   F
F | F |   F
Only true when both P and Q are true.

front: Define the logical connective OR (disjunction) and its truth table.
back:
P | Q | P ∨ Q
T | T |   T
T | F |   T
F | T |   T
F | F |   F
False only when both P and Q are false. This is inclusive OR.

front: What is the truth table for the logical implication (conditional) P → Q?
back:
P | Q | P → Q
T | T |   T
T | F |   F
F | T |   T
F | F |   T
False only when a true premise (P) implies a false conclusion (Q).

front: What is the truth table for the logical connective NOT (negation)?
back:
P | ¬P
T |  F
F |  T
Reverses the truth value of the proposition.

front: What is a tautology, and what is its resulting truth value?
back: A compound proposition that is always true, regardless of the truth values of its constituent propositions. Its truth table column contains only 'T' values.
